description: HTTP responses should use a shared envelope with `id`, top-level `status`, optional `message`, and a shallow `data` payload.
Requests should remain domain-shaped and should not be wrapped in an additional envelope unless correlation metadata becomes necessary later.
Operations with a single authoritative outcome should rely on the top-level `status` rather than duplicating the same status inside `data`.
Collection operations may include per-item statuses inside `data` while keeping the top-level envelope consistent.
The envelope should stay shallow so the same contract maps cleanly to a Go struct with embedded or nested payload data.
